title: شـــراسـة مـنـاخ متـطـرف
summary: الخبير مومن: انجراف التربة سببه أن جبال الريف حديثة التشكل وهشة جيولوجيا أكد عزيز مومن، الخبير في البيئة وعلوم الماء، أن المغرب غير مستعد حاليا لمواجهة ظاهرة التطرف المناخي بحكم أن السدود بنيت في ظروف معينة وفي فترة مناخية عادية، مبرزا أن انجراف التربة الذي شهدته مناطقأكمل القراءة » المقالة شـــراس…
body: الخبير مومن: انجراف التربة سببه أن جبال الريف حديثة التشكل وهشة جيولوجيا أكد عزيز مومن، الخبير في البيئة وعلوم الماء، أن المغرب غير مستعد حاليا لمواجهة ظاهرة التطرف المناخي بحكم أن السدود بنيت في ظروف معينة وفي فترة مناخية عادية، مبرزا أن انجراف التربة الذي شهدته مناطق أكمل القراءة » المقالة شـــراسـة مـنـاخ متـطـرف fمنشورة على جريدة الصباح .

title: Newly visible, city-size 'green comet' will soon be ejected into interstellar space — just like 3I/ATLAS
summary: Comet Wierzchoś, also known as C/2024 E1, is rapidly brightening as it approaches its closest point to Earth next week. But experts predict it will eventually be thrown out of the solar system forever, just like the "alien" comet 3I/ATLAS.

title: Did modern humans wipe out the Neanderthals? New evidence may finally provide answers.
summary: A complex picture of how Neanderthals died out, and the role that modern humans played in their disappearance, is emerging.

title: 'It's telling us there's something big going on': Unprecedented spike in atmospheric methane during the COVID-19 pandemic has a troubling explanation
summary: During the COVID-19 pandemic, the atmosphere temporarily lost its ability to break down methane, leading to a huge spike in the greenhouse gas.

title: Archaeopteryx, one of the world's first proto birds, has a set of weird, never-before-seen features, new study reveals
summary: Iconic transition species between dinosaurs and birds may have had weird 'teeth' on roof of its mouth and a highly mobile tongue, study reveals

title: NASA telescope spots the building blocks for life spewing out of comet 3I/ATLAS
summary: NASA's SPHEREx space telescope detected organic molecules coming off comet 3I/ATLAS as the interstellar visitor made its closest approach to Earth in December.

title: Subterranean tunnel, possibly used for medieval cult rituals, discovered in Stone Age tomb in Germany
summary: A tunnel system discovered in a Stone Age tomb in Germany suggests medieval people created hiding places for their cultic rituals.

title: China banned all fishing to save the Yangtze River. This 'nuclear' option appears to be working.
summary: Decades of overfishing and habitat degradation led to huge declines in freshwater biodiversity in China's longest river, but there are signs of recovery after a fishing ban was implemented in 2021.

title: Astronomers discover 'unique inside-out system' with a rocky planet far from where it belongs
summary: Astronomers have spotted an unusual 'inside-out' planetary system where a rocky world seems to have formed far beyond the realm typically reserved for gas giants.

title: China's emissions are flatlining — and may be falling — in critical turning point for biggest emitter, report says
summary: The carbon emissions of the world's biggest greenhouse gas emitter have plateaued for nearly two years.

title: Antarctica 'ghost particle' observatory gets major upgrade that could 'pave the way' to physics breakthroughs
summary: The National Science Foundation's massive IceCube neutrino detector at the South Pole just got a major new upgrade, which promises to take the search for "ghost particles" to a new level.

title: في فيلم أميركي ضخم.. مشهد عن الأهرامات يثير غضب المصريين
summary: أثار مقطع من واحد من أشهر الأفلام الأميركية هذا العام، استياء عدد كبير من المصريين، بسبب معلومة تتعلق ببناء الأهرامات.

title: Radio signal discovered at the center of our galaxy could put Einstein's relativity to the test
summary: Scientists hope to probe the nature of general relativity through a possible pulsar found in the center of the Milky Way, near a supermassive black hole.

title: Our leading theory of dark matter may be wrong, huge new gravity study hints
summary: New research using a space-time phenomenon predicted by Einstein presents evidence that the invisible backbone of the universe may be much "fuzzier" than we realized.

title: China has planted so many trees around the Taklamakan Desert that it's turned this 'biological void' into a carbon sink
summary: Huge-scale ecological engineering around the edges of one of the world's largest and driest deserts has turned it into a carbon sink that absorbs more CO2 than it emits, research suggests.

title: Something supercharged Uranus with radiation during Voyager flyby 40 years ago. Scientists now know what.
summary: Forty years ago, Voyager 2 flew past Uranus and observed radiation levels that defied explanation. Now, scientists may finally know exactly what happened.

title: CT scans reveal the last moments of Inca children sacrificed as 'messengers to the gods'
summary: New CT scans reveal the last moments of the Inca children who were sacrificed and mummified about 500 years ago.

title: Astronaut snaps salty, pink Valentine's Day 'heart' shining in Argentina — Earth from space
summary: A 2024 astronaut photo shows a striking pink, heart-shaped salt lake in the middle of the Argentine lowlands. The endearing photo was originally released to celebrate Valentine's Day.

title: New 'sungrazing' comet could become visible to the naked eye during the day — if the sun doesn't destroy it
summary: The newly discovered sungrazing comet C/2026 A1 (MAPS) will undergo an extremely close slingshot around our star in early April, and could become bright enough to be seen without a telescope during the day.

title: Impossibly powerful 'ghost particle' that slammed into Earth may have come from an exploding black hole — and it could upend both particle physics and cosmology
summary: A supercharged neutrino that smashed into our planet in 2023 may have been spit out by an exploding primordial black hole with a "dark charge." If true, this theory could lead to a definitive catalog of all subatomic particles and unveil the elusive identity of dark matter.

title: مقتل أشخاص في انهيار مبنى بشمال لبنان.. وبحث متواصل عن ناجين
summary: قُتل خمسة أشخاص على الأقل، الأحد، في مدينة طرابلس في شمال لبنان إثر انهيار مبنى، وفق ما أفاد الإعلام الرسمي، بينما تواصل فرق الإنقاذ البحث عن ناجين.

title: Microbes in Iceland are hoarding nitrogen, and that's mucking up the nutrient cycle
summary: A study in Iceland found that microbes are hoarding more nitrogen for themselves, altering nutrient cycling and leaving less for plants.

title: Physicists push quantum boundaries by turning a superfluid into a supersolid — and back — for the first time
summary: Physicists saw excitons, a type of quasiparticle, undergo a reversible phase transition from superfluid to supersolid for the first time, opening new doors for studying extreme states of matter.

title: Dramatic death of Comet C/2025 K1 (ATLAS) caught on camera — Space photo of the week
summary: The Gemini North telescope snapped a spectacular view of Comet C/2025 K1 (ATLAS) breaking apart as it emerged from the other side of the sun in October.

title: شجاعة استثنائية.. سبح ساعات في عرض البحر لإنقاذ أسرته
summary: أشاد عناصر الإنقاذ الأستراليون بشجاعة فتى مراهق سبح لأربع ساعات وسط الأمواج قبالة سواحل أستراليا بحثا عمّن ينقذ عائلته.

title: لصلته بإبستين.. استقالة سياسي بريطاني من "حزب العمال"
summary: قال السياسي البريطاني بيتر ماندلسون إنه استقال من عضوية حزب العمال لتجنب التسبب للحزب في "مزيد من الإحراج" عقب تكشف معلومات جديدة حول صداقته مع جيفري إبستين.

title: صدمة للدروس الخصوصية.. إقبال على حصص الإنترنت بمصر
summary: تتلقى الدروس الخصوصية صدمة قوية في مصر حاليا، مع تزايد الإقبال بشكل ملحوظ حصص التعليم عبر الإنترنت، والصفحات الخاصة بعدد من مشاهير المعلمين عبر مواقع التواصل الاجتماعي، وموقع "يوتيوب"، والتي تقدم الخدمات المجانية للطلاب من مراحل التعليم المختلفة.

title: دمية "الحصان الباكي" تحصد الإعجاب في الصين
summary: في مدينة ييوو للتجارة الدولية، أكبر سوق للجملة في الصين، يتزاحم الزبائن في متجر صغير بحثا عن سلعة رائجة بشكل غير متوقع قبل حلول السنة القمرية الجديدة.
